goal: Turn a lesson, training module, curriculum fragment, or onboarding flow into a polished short-session browser game that feels motivating instead of corporate or punitive.

primaryMapping.useWhen: The user wants exploration, discovery, collecting concepts, museum-style progression, or a soft-pressure learning loop.

alternativeMappings.useWhen: The ask is closer to quick drills, timed review, or route-based streak play.
The ask is about mastery ladders, chapter climbing, or progressive skill gates.

sessionDesign.lessonUnit: One topic cluster per slice. Do not try to teach an entire course in one screen.
sessionDesign.difficultyCurve: Start with recognition, then recall, then speed or pressure
sessionDesign.failureModel: Soft retry, low frustration, and visible progress after each run

core: Collect correct knowledge markers or checkpoints
Unlock the next concept cluster or badge

contentRules: Make the learning content visible inside the pickups, signs, cards, gates, or checkpoint surfaces.
If the game uses wrong answers, make the mistake readable and recoverable instead of instantly humiliating the player.
Use encouraging progression language such as mastery, streak, chapter, confidence, or badge instead of combat-first framing.

artDirection.look: bright, inviting, reward-rich, clean silhouettes, low-clutter screen composition
artDirection.ui: large readable labels, obvious reward states, no debug-looking HUD
artDirection.fx: pleasant collection bursts, chapter clear surfaces, low-threat failure feedback

assetShotList: learner avatar move and celebrate family
concept pickup family with clear labels or symbols
reward badge, star, or mastery token family
HUD plate, progress meter, streak badge, and chapter clear surface
playtestFocus: is the learning content readable while moving
does the reward loop feel motivating instead of noisy
are mistakes recoverable without killing the session
does the slice still feel like a real game rather than a worksheet
approximationNotes: If the user wants quizzes, branching dialogue, dashboards, or assessment exports, keep the playable loop small and state honestly which parts are still approximated.
Do not fake a full LMS or teacher dashboard inside the first slice.
